verdict_summary: OK — v2.2.3 baseline production sanity confirmed. Korean warning '민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.' rendered with '차단' keyword. RRN decode ABSENT. Matches #802 result on post-v2.2.3 H4d v2 binary, confirming no regression on rollback to v2.2.3 binary (etap-root-260506.sv.debug, PID 2180550).

evidence.warning_text_quote: 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.
evidence.answer_area_state: warning_only — single Korean block message with warning icon (⚠), copy/share buttons present but no AI-generated analysis content. User message shows submitted prompt with RRN '880101-1234567' as typed; AI response area contains ONLY the block warning, no decode/explanation/analysis of the resident registration number.
evidence.console_errors_excerpt: FedCM was disabled either temporarily based on previous user action or permanently via site settings. (index):1; [GSI_LOGGER]: FedCM get() rejects with NetworkError: Error retrieving a token. (2 errors, 11 warnings — all GSI/FedCM related Google Sign-In failures, unrelated to APF block path)

recommendation: PASS — v2.2.3 baseline binary handles chatgpt PII (RRN) correctly. Production rollback to v2.2.3 is safe for chatgpt service. No further action required for this verification.
notes: Test executed on Test PC (Windows 11) via windows-mcp. Workflow: opened new Chrome tab → navigated chatgpt.com → Ctrl+Shift+R hard reload → F12 DevTools → typed RRN prompt → Enter → 8s wait → Snapshot+Console capture. Anonymous (logged-out) chatgpt session used (no AUTH_REQUIRED issue — service responded with the APF-injected warning before any login wall). v2.2.3 binary verified: etap-root-260506.sv.debug, PID 2180550.
